data.title: Shake Shack stock down 16.6% as second quarter adjusted earnings per share beat analyst estimate. Quarterly sales at $356.466M.
data.description: Shares of Shake Shack, Inc. (NYSE: SHAK) have dropped by over 16.6% as of the latest update on Thursday. The burger chain reported adjusted earnings per share of 44 cents for the second quarter, surpassing the analyst consensus of 37 cents. Additionally, quarterly sales amounted to $356.466 million, reflecting a 12.6% increase compared to the previous year and exceeding the Street's anticipated $352.299 million in sales. System-wide sales also experienced significant growth. For full details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CNH Industrial to report Q2 earnings on 08/01, EPS expected at $0.15. Investors hopeful for positive results.
data.description: CNH Industrial (NYSE: CNH) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on August 1, 2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.15 for the company. Investors are anticipating the report, with hopes of CNH Industrial exceeding expectations and providing optimistic guidance for the upcoming quarter. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Growth projected in Application Security Market with 14% CAGR from 2025-2029, reaching USD 55.99 billion by 2025.
data.description: An industry report titled "Application Security Market Opportunity and Future Growth Dynamics" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The global application security market is expected to experience substantial expansion, with projections indicating it will reach a value of USD 55.99 billion by 2025, showcasing a strong annual growth rate of 15.6%. In 2020, the market had already shown promising growth trends.

data.title: SPAR Group survey finds product availability as top factor
data.description: In Auburn Hills, Michigan, a recent study by SPAR Group, Inc. found that product availability is the top consideration for shoppers deciding to make in-store purchases. The research highlights that customers prioritize the presence of products on shelves over other factors. This study underscores the significance of maintaining adequate stock levels and ensuring items are readily accessible to consumers. The results emphasize the crucial role of product availability in influencing shopping decisions, showcasing its paramount importance for retailers.

data.title: NFL Alumni partners with BBB Foundation for Better Business to create Small Business Training Camp for ex-NFL players.
data.description: Today, in Mount Laurel, N.J., the NFL Alumni Association has revealed a new initiative called the Small Business Training Camp, developed in partnership with the BBB Foundation for Better Business. This program aims to offer educational assistance, guidance, and resources to entrepreneurs looking to enhance their small businesses. The collaboration seeks to provide support and tools necessary for small business owners to thrive.

data.title: 5WPR launches consumer PR framework aligning earned media with e-commerce results, enhancing connections.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, a leading independent PR firm in the U.S., 5WPR, unveiled a revamped consumer communications strategy aligned to boost the integration of earned media campaigns with e-commerce outcomes. This initiative aims to enhance direct connectivity between media outreach and online sales performance, signaling a new direction for the firm in optimizing marketing efforts.

data.title: Q2 2025 commercial lines premium rates in Canada decreased with 3.63% increase, down from 5.83% in Q2 2024.
data.description: Applied Systems® released the Q2 2025 results of the Applied Commercial Index™ for the Canadian insurance industry. In this quarter, the average premium rate increases decreased to 3.63%, a notable drop from 5.83% in Q2 2024. All lines of business exhibited declines compared to the previous year. The average renewal rate change reduced across various commonly placed Commercial Lines categories such as Real Estate Property, Business and Professional Services, and Construction. Conversely, Hospitality Services and Retail Services saw an uptick in average renewal rate change. These insights and more can be found on Benzinga.com.

JPMORGAN CEO JAMIE DIMON: FED SHOULD BE CAUTIOUS ON RATE DECISIONS Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan Chase, urged a measured and cautious approach to interest rate decisions, warning that markets may be underestimating inflation risks and overestimating the likelihood of rate cuts.

data.title: ATDM program in Danville, Va. celebrates 1,000th graduate, Glenn Claypool, after completion of 16-week training program.
data.description: ATDM in Danville, Virginia, marked a milestone as they celebrated the completion of their 1,000th program participant. The success of the Accelerated Training in Defense Manufacturing program was emphasized, showcasing its effectiveness in developing the defense workforce. Glenn Claypool was acknowledged as the 1,000th individual to complete the program, where he and other students commemorated finishing the intensive 16-week training. Claypool, who had a background in business management and sales, found ATDM after obtaining his bachelor's degree.

data.title: I.M.F. raises global growth forecast as U.S. reduces tariffs, but warns trade tensions still threaten economy.
data.description: The International Monetary Fund has cautioned that ongoing trade tensions pose a significant risk to the global economy.

data.title: Bitcoin's growth is evident on mainstream platforms like Finviz. Kazakhstan explores investing in Bitcoin. Large ETF funds, central banks show interest. Institutional investment rises with funds like IBIT.US from BlackRock.
data.description: Bitcoin has recently emerged on mainstream platforms such as Finviz, a website listing U.S. stock exchange assets, where it is now displayed alongside traditional currencies like the dollar, euro, and yen. Despite being relatively new compared to these currencies, Bitcoin's presence signals its growing importance in the financial market. Over the past two years, Bitcoin has shown a strong upward trend, attracting not only private investors but also institutional investors like ETF funds and global central banks. For instance, Kazakhstan is considering investing some of its reserves in Bitcoin. Additionally, there are funds like iBit from BlackRock actively investing in Bitcoin, providing easier access to the cryptocurrency through standard shares traded on exchanges. This trend indicates a broader acceptance and integration of Bitcoin into the traditional financial system.

data.title: June CPI data shows inflation rise due to Trump tariffs, impacting S&P 500 negatively.
data.description: In June, the consumer price index indicated a rise in inflation due to Trump's tariffs, marking an impact on the market. The S&P 500 saw a decrease as a result.
